libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Keep or add JVM/native-image test setup equivalent to `--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED` when exercising Guice method interception, proxy generation, or other CGLIB-backed paths. Generate tests against public Guice APIs such as modules, injectors, bindings, scopes, providers, TypeLiteral, InjectionPoint, and method interceptors rather than the stale 2.1.7 internal class names.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8858 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.sonatype.sisu:sisu-guice:2.9.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Existing 2.1.7 tests import internal classes that are absent or renamed in 2.9.1, such as unprefixed `com.google.inject.internal.cglib.*` classes and old `com.google.inject.internal.util.MapMaker`/`Finalizable*` classes.
Without `--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED`, CGLIB-backed paths fail on JDK 25 with module access errors around `ClassLoader#defineClass`.
Optional `org.slf4j:slf4j-api` is declared optional/dynamically imported and is not required for core meaningful coverage.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency or Docker image is needed: sisu-guice 2.9.1 carries its shaded CGLIB/ASM code and has only normal transitive javax.inject/aopalliance dependencies. Meaningful AOP/proxy coverage on JDK 25 does require opening java.lang.
